description:
You are on-call as an operations engineer and need to do a very quick, CLI-only triage of a burst of incident alerts that were captured in a CSV log file.

Source data  
• Absolute path: /home/user/data/service_incidents.log  
• Format: plain UTF-8, comma-separated, **six columns per line** in the following order  
  1. id  
  2. timestamp (ISO-8601, Zulu)  
  3. service (lower-case, dash-separated)  
  4. host  
  5. severity (INFO | WARNING | CRITICAL)  
  6. message (free text; may itself contain spaces)  
• The very first line is a header with the same six column names.

Goal  
Using only the traditional text-processing utilities that specialise in column extraction and recombination (cut, paste, sort, uniq, head, tail, tr, wc, etc. — **do not** rely on awk, sed, Perl, Python, jq, or similar), generate three artefacts:

1. Directory: /home/user/output  
   If it does not exist, create it.

2. Summary TSV: /home/user/output/incident_summary.tsv  
   • Tab-delimited (ASCII 0x09) — **no spaces** used as separators.  
   • Must contain exactly eleven (11) lines:  
     – Line 1 is a header that reads precisely (case-sensitive)  
       timestamp<TAB>service<TAB>severity  
     – Lines 2-11 reproduce columns 2, 3 and 5 of every *data* row in the original CSV, **in the same order they appear in the source file**.  
     – Do not include any of the other CSV columns.  
     – Retain the original timestamp formatting; do not wrap fields in quotes.  
   The automated grader will check:  
     a) file exists at the expected path,  
     b) it has 11 lines,  
     c) the first line matches the required header exactly,  
     d) field delimiters are single tab characters,  
     e) the timestamps on line 2 and line 11 should be “2023-08-14T10:15:32Z” and “2023-08-14T10:40:24Z”, respectively.

3. Critical-service list: /home/user/output/critical_services.txt  
   • One service name per line, no header, **no trailing blank line**.  
   • Populate only the unique service values whose severity in the source log is CRITICAL.  
   • Sort the list alphabetically (byte sort, i.e. the default `sort` behaviour).  
   • The file must contain exactly three (3) lines.

4. Triage audit log: /home/user/output/triage_audit.log  
   • Two lines only, each line an absolute path created in this task, in the following order:  
     Line 1 → /home/user/output/incident_summary.tsv  
     Line 2 → /home/user/output/critical_services.txt  
   • No additional information, timestamps, or commentary.

Constraints & hints  
• You do not have sudo.  
• Work only inside /home/user.  
• The tasks can be completed in 5–10 shell commands when using cut, paste, sort, uniq, and standard redirection.  
• The grader will fail the task if any of the files contain extra whitespace, additional columns, quoted fields, or an unexpected number of lines.  
• Whenever an intermediate step is needed, use temporary files inside /home/user/output and delete them afterwards; the grader ignores any tmp files that remain.  

When you are finished, you should have exactly these three deliverables in /home/user/output, with the precise formats described above. The automated test harness will verify their presence and content.
truth:
Initial setup done by the harness BEFORE the agent starts:

1. Directory structure
   /home/user/data  (0755, owned by user)
   /home/user/output does NOT exist yet.

2. File: /home/user/data/service_incidents.log  (0644, UTF-8)
   Exact contents:
id,timestamp,service,host,severity,message
1001,2023-08-14T10:15:32Z,auth-api,auth01,CRITICAL,Authentication failure rate above threshold
1002,2023-08-14T10:17:05Z,billing-api,billing02,WARNING,Slow response time detected
1003,2023-08-14T10:18:44Z,auth-api,auth02,CRITICAL,Multiple 5xx errors observed
1004,2023-08-14T10:22:11Z,search-api,search01,INFO,Zero-downtime deployment completed
1005,2023-08-14T10:25:53Z,billing-api,billing01,CRITICAL,Database connection pool exhausted
1006,2023-08-14T10:27:19Z,auth-api,auth03,WARNING,Increased latency
1007,2023-08-14T10:30:07Z,search-api,search02,CRITICAL,Out of memory killed process
1008,2023-08-14T10:35:41Z,user-service,user01,INFO,Refresh tokens rotated
1009,2023-08-14T10:38:00Z,billing-api,billing03,CRITICAL,Payment gateway timeout
1010,2023-08-14T10:40:24Z,user-service,user02,WARNING,Cache miss rate increased

Expected artefacts after the agent completes the task:

A. /home/user/output/incident_summary.tsv (0644)
   Exact 11 lines, tab-delimited:
timestamp	service	severity
2023-08-14T10:15:32Z	auth-api	CRITICAL
2023-08-14T10:17:05Z	billing-api	WARNING
2023-08-14T10:18:44Z	auth-api	CRITICAL
2023-08-14T10:22:11Z	search-api	INFO
2023-08-14T10:25:53Z	billing-api	CRITICAL
2023-08-14T10:27:19Z	auth-api	WARNING
2023-08-14T10:30:07Z	search-api	CRITICAL
2023-08-14T10:35:41Z	user-service	INFO
2023-08-14T10:38:00Z	billing-api	CRITICAL
2023-08-14T10:40:24Z	user-service	WARNING

B. /home/user/output/critical_services.txt (0644)
   Exact 3 lines:
auth-api
billing-api
search-api

C. /home/user/output/triage_audit.log (0644)
   Exact 2 lines:
/home/user/output/incident_summary.tsv
/home/user/output/critical_services.txt
